manual_recovery_notes: Chained on the SAME pod as the LoRA manual recovery (pod b1iol4pu1i0wvl) per memory entry 'multi-rung-pod-reuse-over-disposal'. Saved 1 stock-roulette event + image-pull + setup overhead. Launched 'python scripts/train_rung.py --rung full_ft --checkpoint-staging-root /root/training_staging' in nohup at 19:23 UTC immediately after LoRA's '[resume] ALL DONE' marker (19:22:12 UTC). X9 fix (checkpoint output_dir staged on /root, copied to /workspace after training) survived ALL 12 cells without a single FUSE hang. Final marker '[train_rung] DONE rung=full_ft 12 cells / 24 parquets in 3854.3s' at 20:27 UTC. wall_time_sec = 3854.3 (training time) which closely tracks pod-billed time for this rung. estimated_cost = 3854.3/3600 * $1.49 = $1.60 (rounded $1.61). Empty checkpoint dirs left on /workspace because configs/rungs/full_ft.yaml has cleanup_intermediate_checkpoints=true per ADR-019 storage discipline (predictions parquets are the audit artifact; weights would be ~150MB each + ~1.8GB optimizer.pt each). Full-FT trained noticeably faster than LoRA (avg 3.3-4.1 it/s vs LoRA's 2-3 it/s) because there's no adapter forward-pass overhead.
